element_introduction_order.element: Basic three-button control scheme (grab, attack, jump)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns opponent patterns in first CPU match
Strategic finishing tool learned through progression
Partner AI cooperation and tag mechanics
Endgame loop defending against full roster

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-based pacing with escalating difficulty through tournament progression; each bout builds tension through opponent AI aggression, culminating in boss encounters and championship defense mode

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory matches against roster with basic AI; player learns control scheme and character move sets
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive opponent difficulty increase through tournament progression; varied wrestler movesets require adaptive strategy
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss wrestlers (Jumbo, Scorpion) present significant difficulty spike; championship belt defense mode loops entire roster with accumulated experience from opponent patterns
notable_spikes: First boss wrestler encounter (Jumbo or Scorpion depending on story progression)
Championship belt defense mode initiation requiring defense against full roster
Team Battle Royale mode introduces two-on-one AI coordination complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Single Match mode presents sequential one-on-one opponents building toward championship. Victory conditions (pin or submission) remain consistent; opponent rosters vary and increase in difficulty. This is the primary campaign structure.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Single Match mode from match 1 through final boss

notable_level_patterns.description: Non-playable wrestlers (Jumbo Flapjack, Scorpion) serve as final opponents in tournament. These characters have enhanced AI, increased life pools, and more aggressive special attack usage. Defeat unlocks championship belt.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Final matches of Single Match tournament

notable_level_patterns.description: Post-tournament mode where player must defend championship belt against series of re-matched opponents. Creates extended endgame content with familiar opponent patterns but heightened stakes.

Alternative game mode supporting up to four players (2v2 format). Introduces tag mechanics and partner AI coordination. Can be played with CPU partners or human players for extended multiplayer content.
